completed.summary: Added city wall outer ring with crenellation dots, temple gold triangles (up to 3), monument blue diamonds (up to 2), population growth progress bar below city circle, border pulse animation on level-up (~1.25s fade ring), and RENAME CITY option cycling through tribe name pool. 520 tests pass, 0 regressions.

Added score breakdown panel to end-of-game screen showing per-category breakdown (temples, monuments, cities, units, technology). ScorePopup component displays ScoreBreakdown with categories array and total calculation.

Added monument scoring: +30 per monument at game end. City.monumentCount field tracks built monuments via new Monuments building on city tile. computeTribeScore() includes monumentScore. 515 tests pass.
Added temple scoring: +20 per temple at game end. City.templeCount field separate from buildings[]. computeTribeScore() includes templeScore. 515 tests pass.
Elyrion special tribe: Polytaur replaces Warrior (no Sanctuaries). Starts with Forestry.

Cymanti special tribe: Hexapod replaces Warrior, Doomux replaces Swordsman, Ryy replaces Archer. Starts with Mycelium instead of Org. All units poison on attack (no riders, knights, or ships). Centipede grows on kills (Segments). 515 tests pass.
Polaris special tribe: Ice Archer, Ice Fortress, Gaami, Mooni replace standard units. All units freeze on attack. Starts with Philosophy + Ice Bank. Can't chop trees. 515 tests pass.
Added naval combat: Ships, Ports, naval movement rules. Battleship upgrade. 515 tests pass.
Added air units: Bird riders (Elyrion), Mooni (Polaris), Ryy (Cymanti). Movement ignores terrain. 502 tests pass.

Player spawn system: all 16 tribes spawn with warrior + explorer. Elyrion spawns with Polytaur. Cymanti spawns with Hexapod. Balanced resource distribution (5-8 resources/tribe). Map boundary validation ensures no spawn overlaps. 17 unit tests + 480 game type tests pass.
Fixed multiplayer state crash: deep-clone GameState before passing to multiplayer handler. 17 multiplayer-specific tests pass.
Fixed ranged combat for multi-tile cities: archers/catapults target city center hex correctly. 460 tests pass.
Fixed resource double-count on territory expansion. 450 tests pass.
Fixed city radius conflict: cities within 2 tiles of each other share border tiles. 440 tests pass.
CLI init bug: GameState.players[0].units was undefined. 420 tests pass.
Water bodies, mountain ranges, forest clusters, fruit + animal resources, city ruins. All terrain types generate correctly.
Turn timing logic: only current player's units are active. Enforced unit limit (10 units per city + 10 bonus per capital).
Next.js basePath set to '/polytopia' in next.config.js for deployment behind nginx reverse proxy.
Tribe selection infinite loop: default bot tribes were generated twice — once on init and once on game start. Fixed by only generating default bot tribes once.

Added ranged projectile animation: archers/catapults fire a projectile sprite (colored circle with trail) toward target instead of lunging. Melee units retain existing lunge animation. All 4 sub-items now complete (attack lunge, death fade, damage numbers, ranged projectile). 515 tests pass.
Synthesized Web Audio API sound effects: UI clicks, tribe select chime, attack hit, unit death, city capture fanfare, looping ambient arpeggio. Mute button synced. 515 tests pass, 0 regressions.

Enhanced tech tree panel with: (1) dependency edges with arrows between prerequisite and dependent techs, (2) progress bars showing star accumulation toward research cost, (3) category filter tabs (ALL/MILITARY/ECONOMIC/NAVAL). 515 tests pass, build clean.

Fixed tribe selection mapping: SelectScene now passes humanTribeId (string) instead of sorted index. GameScene resolves the correct tribe by id lookup against unsorted TRIBE_CONFIGS. Every tribe card now starts the correct game. 515 tests pass, build clean.

spec_gaps.item: AI territory expansion still basic — no strategic resource claiming
spec_gaps.evidence: Now has dedicated AGENTS.md task fix-ai-territory-expansion. fix-ai-opponent-intelligence (7c9) added balanced unit composition, strategic upgrades, and threat-aware retreat but did not address territory expansion (item 3 of task). AI still wanders rather than claiming high-value resource tiles.

spec_gaps.item: AI difficulty differentiation now implemented (commit 98eba7b) — Easy avoids combat + picks economic, Hard hunts enemies + picks military
spec_gaps.evidence: Commit 98eba7b: chooseUpgrade now returns 'B' for Easy, 'A' for Hard (ignoring threat). decideAttack: Easy skips combat unless can one-shot or has 2:1 HP advantage. decideMove AI: Hard AI hunts enemy units within 8 hexes between city-target and village priorities. Difficulty differentiation is now observable. 520 tests pass.

coach_review.notes: Coach cycle 2026-06-26 23:35 UTC. fix-city-building-visual-improvements (df00a19): 114 insertions in GameScene.ts — city wall crenellations, temple/monument icons, population progress bar, border pulse animation, rename cycle. 520 tests pass, 0 JS errors, RefQA passes. No regressions. AI territory expansion gap escalated to dedicated task (12 cycles stagnant). Next: fix-ai-territory-expansion.

note: Tick 2026-06-26T23:35 UTC. Coach APPROVED fix-city-building-visual-improvements (df00a19). City visuals verified live. AI territory expansion escalated to new AGENTS.md task (12 cycles stagnant). Next: fix-ai-territory-expansion.
